channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israeli strikes kill at least 15 in Qana, a Lebanese town with a dark history of civilian deaths
item.description: Israeli strikes have killed at least 15 people in the southern Lebanese town of Qana, which has long been associated with civilian deaths after Israeli strikes during previous conflicts with Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In the heartland of Mexico's Sinaloa cartel, the old ways have changed and violence rages
item.description: Cellphone chats have become death sentences in the continuing, bloody factional war inside the Sinaloa drug cartel.
item.dc:creator: Mark Stevenson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Set sail on world’s largest cruise ship, which can accommodate over 7,000 guests per voyage
item.description: Icon of the Seas is the world's largest cruise ship. With extensive dining options, record-breaking pools and breathtaking live shows, there's something for everyone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: SEE IT: Suspect discovered hiding in couch on police bodycam while serving arrest warrant
item.description: Bodycam footage from police in the UK show a game of hide-and-seek that ended poorly for one suspect being served an arrest warrant over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah vows to expand attacks in Israel after deadly strike in Lebanon's Christian heartland
item.description: AITO, Lebanon (AP) — The day after a deadly Israeli airstrike in northern Lebanon – far from Hezbollah’s main area of influence – the militant group's acting leader said it would aim rockets into more areas of Israel.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russian man rescued after 67 days adrift at sea. His family members didn't make it.
item.description: A Russian man was rescued in the Sea of Okhotsk after surviving for two months adrift in an inflatable boat without engine power, but his brother and nephew died.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: All about National Archaeological Museum in Athens, one of the world’s most prominent museums of its kind
item.description: The National Archaeological Museum houses artifacts dating back thousands of years. The collections are full of sculptures, artworks and more for guests to explore.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia releases man whose daughter's drawing opposed Ukraine fighting
item.description: A group that monitors political detentions in Russia says that a man convicted of discrediting the military after his daughter made a drawing criticizing Russia’s military actions in Ukraine has been released from prison after serving 22 months.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hereditary nobles have sat in Britain's Parliament for centuries. Their time may be up
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Like his ancestors for centuries, the Earl of Devon serves in Parliament, helping to make the laws of the land. But not for much longer.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US troops arrive In Israel to help operate missile defense system
item.description: The first team of U.S. military personnel arrived in Israel on Monday to help with the installment and operation of a Terminal High-Altitude Area Defense (THAAD) battery, which aims to boost Israeli air defenses amid threats from Iran. Pentagon press secretary Maj. Gen.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Violence-hit Pakistan locks down the capital for an Asian security meeting
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Shaken by multiple militant attacks, Pakistani authorities have locked down the capital in a major security move as senior officials from several nations arrive for an Asian security group meeting.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea blows up parts of inter-Korean road and rail links in a symbolic display of anger
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — In a symbolic display of anger, North Korea on Tuesday blew up the northern sections of unused road and rail routes that once linked it with South Korea, with the rivals exchanging threats days after the North claimed that the South flew drones over its capital Pyongyang.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Conkers controversy: World tournament investigates claims of cheating with steel chestnut
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The World Conker Championships said Tuesday that it was investigating allegations of cheating after this year's men’s winner was found to have a steel chestnut in his pocket.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Southern Africa is enduring its worst hunger crisis in decades due to El Niño, the UN says
item.description: The United Nations' food agency says months of drought in southern Africa triggered by the El Niño weather phenomenon have had a devastating impact on more than 27 million people and caused the region’s worst hunger crisis in decades.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Imray, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A pastry brought to Mexico by British miners is still popular after 200 years
item.description: REAL DEL MONTE, Mexico (AP) — Isabel Arriaga Lozano carefully fills a small pastry with a savory mix of meat, potatoes and chili pepper. She is crafting a “paste” (pronounced PAH-stay), a beloved Mexican snack with a rich history.
item.dc:creator: INDIA GRANT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China deploys record 125 warplanes in large-scale military drill in warning to Taiwan
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China employed a record 125 aircraft, as well as its Liaoning aircraft carrier and ships, in large-scale military exercises surrounding Taiwan and its outlying islands Monday, simulating the sealing off of key ports in a move that underscores the tense situation in the Taiwan S
item.dc:creator: JOHNSON LAI and HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN Security Council voices 'strong concern' for UN peacekeepers after Israeli attacks
item.description: It's the first statement by the powerful U.N. body since Israel's attacks on the positions of the peacekeeping force.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez criticizes Biden administration over 'unrestrained' Israel government
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) criticized the Biden administration on Monday over what she called an “unrestrained” Israeli government.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Why US is sending troops, prized air defense system to Israel
item.description: The U.S. announced this week a major new air defense system for Israel, along with the deployment of 100 American troops to operate it in the country, in a development that will bolster Israeli security but potentially put American forces at risk.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: U.S. to send troops and air defense system to Israel
item.description: The Pentagon announced the U.S. military will send a Terminal High Altitude Defense battery and about 100 U.S. troops to Israel.
item.dc:creator: Vinay Simlot, WGN 9

item.title: Pandas en route to National Zoo after a year
item.description: Two new giant pandas are reportedly en route to the National Zoo in Washington, D.C. “Something *giant* is coming to Washington, D.C. via the @FedEx Panda Express!” the zoo said in a post on social media platform X on Monday. “The Zoo will be closed to the public tomorrow, Oct. 15.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Hezbollah's drones are a fierce and evasive threat to Israel
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — One of the worst mass casualty strikes on Israel in a year of war came not from dozens of Iranian ballistic missiles nor the repeated barrages of rocket fire launched by Hamas and Hezbollah. Instead, it was a single drone.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Poland's leader defends his decision to suspend the right to asylum
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Poland's Prime Minister Donald Tusk on Monday defended a plan to suspend the right to asylum as human rights and civil society organizations argued that fundamental rights must be respected.
item.dc:creator: VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia says it captured a southern Ukraine village in a push before winter comes
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia said Monday it captured the village of Levadne in southern Ukraine as it probes for weaknesses along the war ’s roughly 1,000-kilometer (600-mile) front line, including in eastern areas that are the main focus of Moscow’s military effort before winter arrives.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vial that contained nerve agent that killed UK woman contained enough poison to kill thousands
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The lead counsel for a public inquiry into the 2018 death of a British woman poisoned by a Soviet-developed nerve agent said Monday that there was enough poison in the vial she unwittingly opened to kill thousands of people.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: India-Canada diplomatic war intensifies over killing probe
item.description: India has strongly rejected Canada's allegations that its diplomats are "persons of interest" in a murder investigation, stating that the claims are part of Trudeau's "political agenda" and that Canada had failed to provide evidence to support its claims.
item.dc:creator: Sarakshi Rai, The Hill

item.title: UNIFIL ignored Hezbollah terror build up for 18 years, Israel's UN ambassador says
item.description: Amid fighting between Israel and Hezbollah terrorists, the U.N. is facing criticism that its peacekeeping force has failed to stop the Iran-terror proxy's military build up in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Koreans fighting in Ukraine alongside Russians, Zelensky says
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said Sunday that North Korean troops are now fighting alongside Russian forces in Ukraine, appearing to confirm reports that have been circulating in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Maybe advanced civilizations aren’t all that advanced: Scientist
item.description: Antonio Gelis-Filho writes that perhaps advanced civilizations have reached the limits of their technology, and we may be close to doing the same.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Harris urges Israel to ramp up Gaza aid, citing UN food report
item.description: Vice President Harris on Sunday pushed for Israel to ramp up aid to Gaza, citing a World Food Programme (WFP) report. “The UN reports that no food has entered northern Gaza in nearly 2 weeks.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: North Korea sends warning to South Korea, saying troops ready to strike if more drones appear
item.description: North Korea said its army units are prepared to launch strikes against South Korea if another drone carrying propaganda flyers flies into the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran making billions in oil revenues under Biden admin as expert slams sanctions policy for lack of 'pressure'
item.description: Some critics have claimed the revenues do not paint an accurate picture due to the fluctuation of prices and lack of reliable sources, but an expert argued that other reports confirm an export surge.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Columbus remains, verified after 500 years, show he was Jewish: documentary
item.description: A 22-year investigation by Spanish forensic researchers concluded that Christopher Columbus was a Sephardic Jew from Western Europe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Heavy Israeli bombardment in northern Gaza as UN peacekeepers in Lebanon are hit again
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Palestinians in northern Gaza described heavy Israeli bombardment Saturday in the hours after airstrikes killed at least 22 people, as Israel warned people there and in southern Lebanon to get out of the way of offensives against the Hamas and Hezbollah militant groups.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Yemen’s Houthi rebels attack a Liberian-flagged ship in the Red Sea
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels attacked a Liberian-flagged chemical tanker on Thursday in the Red Sea, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: “We also sent equipment at the beginning of the pandemic,” Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said. “But about the phone calls — it’s not true.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Google loses final EU court appeal against 2.4 billion euro fine in antitrust shopping case
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Google lost its final legal challenge on Tuesday against a European Union penalty for giving its own shopping recommendations an illegal advantage over rivals in search results, ending a long-running antitrust case that came with a whopping fine.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation
